Management discontinue the practice of paying expenses on reimbursement basis; instead, payment shall be made by issuing checks directly to the creditors or suppliers.

Management through the Municipal Accountant demands from the contractors the amount of liquidated damages for contracts which are not completed within the contract time as provided in Section 8.1 of RA 9184.

Office of the Municipal Engineer properly monitors the implementation of projects to avoid delay and ensure its completion.
Management investigates the causes for the missing/unaccounted PPE items including those not found and identify the persons deemed liable thereof.

The Municipal Mayor to requires the Municipal Treasurer to enforce strict implementation of the collections and remittances procedures by establishing proper internal control over the management of government fund.  

Revenue Collecting Officers complies with the provisions of Section 123 and 124 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) 1445 and Section 470 (d) (2) of Republic Act (R.A) 7160.

Management effects the immediate implementation of fund transfers/grants and the submission of liquidation reports to the concerned source agencies, remit the remaining balances of the funds after the completion of the project and/or effect any adjustments, if warranted, unless otherwise stipulated in the Memorandum of Agreement that unused funds shall no longer be required to be returned to the source agency and can be used for other projects of the LGU.

Municipal Accountant sends written demands to the individuals with existing unliquidated cash advances for settlement/ liquidation and issue certifications on accounts with no available documents in accordance with COA Memo 2017-010.

The Municipal Treasurer updates properly and completely the posting of all transactions, including adjustments/correcting entries, in the cashbooks so that the cash accountability for a given period can be readily determined.  

Periodic reconciliation of the MTO and OMA records shall be done.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record supplies and materials purchased in bulk in accordance with the existing accounting rules and regulations pursuant to Sections 51 and 121, Volume I of the MNGAS for LGUs in order to ensure fair presentation and accountability.  It is also recommended that the GSO Designate and staff properly monitor and control the custodianship and utilization of supplies in the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting journal entries to correct the amount of depreciation expense and accumulated depreciation for the current year. It is also recommended that prior years’ depreciation expense be recorded as prior years’ adjustments.

We recommended the following courses of action:
The Office of the Municipal Engineer coordinate with the Office of the Municipal Accountant as to the status of implementation of projects implemented through grants.
The Office of the Municipal Accountant trace the sources of the accounts listed under the Due to NGAs and determine their respective status of implementation.
Liquidate all implemented and/or completed projects and submit the necessary supporting documents to the source agency.
The Office of the Municipal Accountant shall book the corresponding asset account for completed projects and recognize income through Prior Year Adjustments.
For excess funds, the same shall be returned to the source agency as soon as possible.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the adjusting journal entries to record as expense/prior period adjustment the issuance of Semi-Expandable Properties to end-users, and, provide a copy of the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher to the COA Auditor for verification. Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant use the list of new accounts provided by COA Circular No. 2024-006 in recording Semi-Expendable Properties acquired and issued to end-users moving forward.

We recommended that management submit the lacking documents for proper verification. Moreover, management has to prepare its liquidation reports and related documentation in accordance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445 to ensure transparency in all municipal transactions.

We recommended the following courses of action:
Submit the Transmittal Letter/s stamped with “Received” by the COA ICFAU.
Submit a written explanation as to why the Resolution authorizing the release of the Confidential Funds do not indicate 2/3 votes of the members of the Municipal Peace and Order Council, and submit any document to support the 2/3 votes requirement.
Submit a written explanation as to the reason why specific monthly/quarterly targets were not indicated in the Physical and Financial Plan on the Confidential Fund of the LGU and submit any equivalent document showing the targets on the CF of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer closely monitor the expiration dates of the collector’s fidelity bond and facilitate the renewal of the bonds before the expiration dates. We also recommended that collectors, who have yet to apply for bond shall not be allowed to perform collection functions. Lastly, the Municipal Treasurer shall submit a copy of the Confirmation Letter from the Bureau of Treasury for each accountable officers whose bonds have already expired and the newly applied ones to the COA as soon as possible for verification.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer designate permanent employees only as Revenue Collecting Officers.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer monitor the collections and ensure that deposits of the Dive Camp collections to the LGU’s depository bank are intact daily.
We recommended that management implement its Solid Waste Management Plan. Further, it is recommended that the LGU allocate a budget for the repair and maintenance of its shredder machine.

We recommended that the municipality ensure that the 20% Development Fund be optimally utilized to help attain desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes providing utmost benefits to the barangay constituents.
We recommended that management stop the practice of charging recurring expenses amounting to more than ₱1,000.00 to the Petty Cash Fund in accordance with Section 48 of the MNGAS, Volume 1.

enumerated in DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 annually and ensure that the
amount appropriated for LCPC is fully utilized during the year it is appropriated.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer stop the practice of having the DVs be brought by the payees, and submit reports on time. Require the Accountant to submit reports to COA on or before the date prescribed by aforecited regulations.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The financial statements, 2016 together with supporting schedules for CY 2016 were submitted on 2/14/18 but only the BRS transmitted to Municipal Office on April 18, 2017.
observations.reason: Other supporting reports and schedules such as Ageing Schedules, subsidiary ledgers and Schedule of PPE and the Notes to the FS were submitted late.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the subsequent SMR be dated after the determination that it is in conformity with PPSAS, and reflect the amounts that are based on best estimates and informed judgment of management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management enforce strict compliance with Section 361.e(2) of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM) Volume I by immediately marking all LGU vehicles “For Official Use Only”.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the concerned personnel of the Supply Unit to immediately renew all Acknowledgement Receipts of Equipment (AREs) and to immediately cancel old AREs and issue new AREs in the name of the new end-user when equipment is transferred.

observations.reason: MGSO still find it difficult to locate the AREs issued in 2015 and prior years.

observations.reason: Of the 1,216 unaccounted forms reported, 314 pcs were still to be reported/accounted as of 12/31/17.

observations.reason: However, its projects and programs were incorporated in the appropriations of the different offices. For 2018 appropriations were separated.

observations.recommendation: a) We recommend further that Management locate all vouchers as these disbursements otherwise these vouchers will become permanently disallowed; b) We recommend further that Management submit the DVs for the stated disbursements or else these will be permanently disallowed.

ND No. 2015-001-100-(13) dtd 1/23/15 in the amount of P440,777.50 is still not settled.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant and other concerned municipal officials to adhere strictly to the afore-cited provisions in the required submission of financial reports, statements and other records at the required timeframe.

observations.reason: CY 2016 year-end FS were not submitted on time. Reiterated in Finding No. 1 of Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to submit an explanation, under oath, for the former’s failure to attach ORs in the RCD, and the latter for his recording the collection despite the absence of ORs to support the entry made on the books that formed part of the revenue in the Statement of Financial Performance as of December 31, 2015.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 None
observations.reason: Proper procedures are yet to be finalized by the concerned municipal employees. 2016 transactions were not yet compliant to recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management use the 20% Development Fund only for projects and programs prescribed under DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-01.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Not Implemented The LGU lacked funds thus, the 20% DF was only utilized for projects and programs.
observations.reason: The LGU lacked funds; thus, the 20% DF was utilized for other purposes.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer stop the practice of having the
DVs be brought by the payees, and submit reports on time. Require the Municipal
Accountant to submit reports to COA on or before the date prescribed by
aforecited regulations.

We recommend that subsequent SMR for the LGU’s FS be dated after the
determination that it is in conformity with PPSAS, and reflect the amounts that are
based on best estimates and informed judgment of management.

We recommend to the Municipal Treasurer to update properly and
completely the posting of all transactions, including adjustments/correcting entries,
in the cashbook so that the cash accountability for a given period can be readily
determined.

We recommend to management that Collecting Officers be required to turn
over their collections intact daily or not later than the next working day to the
Liquidating Officer pursuant to Section 31, Volume I of the NGAS Manual.
Moreover, require the aforementioned Collectors to immediately remit their
collections in the total amount of P32,787.56.

We recommend that management enforce strict compliance with Section
361.e(2) of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM) Volume I
by immediately marking all vehicles “For Official Use Only” and the name of the
Office clearly stated under such markings.

We recommend that management instruct the concerned personnel of the
Supply Unit to immediately renew all Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment
(ARE) and every three (3) years thereafter pursuant to Section 56 of the NGAS
Manual, so that the responsibility over the loss of any of these properties could be
readily determined. In cases when the equipment is transferred from one employee
to another, the old ARE shall be cancelled and returned to former holder/user of the
equipment and a new ARE shall be accomplished in the name of the new end-user of
the property.

We recommend that in view of the SC’s decision pronouncing the
unconstitutionality of the PDAF, the remaining funds, in the amount of P 25,300.00,
shall be returned to the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) so that it
can be used for the other priority programs of the government.

We recommend the management to assess its existing organizational
structure and staffing pattern so that the necessary positions be filled-up and
plantilla personnel be appointed, thereby, avoiding the hiring of excessive number
of job order/casuals and contractual employees; and as well, hiring them on the
basis of their true necessity to avoid waste of much needed government funds.

We recommend that the Accounting Sections be directed to update and reconcile their respective records in order to ascertain the correctness and accuracy of valuation of PPE in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 RCPPE for CY 2015 was submitted to COA. The report was already reconciled with the books of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer to perform immediate review/checking of the subject unaccountable forms, produce immediately the Report of missing accountable forms, render report to this Office for audit and review.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The MT submitted initial report on the subject. Out of the reported 1,216 unaccounted forms (without money value), 723 were reported/accounted leaving a balance of 493 pieces.
observations.reason: Time constraint and lack of personnel who can focus on the work.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Accountant to make representations with the depository banks so that reconciling items found in the bank records could be made in order to reconcile both records and effect the adjustments on the books to reflect the correct balance of the asset account and the fair presentation of the financial statements at year end.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Journal entries were already made to effect the said reconciling items based on available documents.
observations.reason: Journal entries made to effect the said reconciling items based on available documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer to ascertain whether the checks were claimed or not.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Management require the Municipal Treasurer to ascertain whether the checks were claimed or not. Demand letters were served. Management informed that actions were already made.
observations.reason: BRS verified and found in order.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit DVs for cash advances for Intelligence/Confidential Expenses in adherence to COA/DBM/DILG/GCC/DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The CA of the 1st quarter of CY 2017 were submitted to COA ATL for post-audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of allowing replenishment of petty cash fund at amounts higher than the established/reduced amount and adhere to the provisions of Section 48 and 52 of Volume I of the NGAS Manual in the replenishment as well as in the recording of Petty Cash Fund by implementing the Imprest Fund System.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Recommendations of the COA were applied by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly observe the rules and regulations provided for in COA Circular No. 2007-002 & Sections 27 and 52 of the NGAS Manual regarding granting and recording of cash advances for traveling officials to avoid erroneous journal entries and possible loss and misuse of government funds; and further effect the correcting entry shown above.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Cash advances for other officials and employees of the LGU were not delegated to the disbursing officer.
observations.reason: The LGU officials and disbursing personnel were already.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management stop the practice of granting cash advances to the disbursing officer alone and require the Disbursing Officer, through the Municipal Treasurer, request for a secured cash on vault inside the workplace to ensure proper safekeeping of government funds in conformity with Section 101(1) and 105 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Management has included in their next budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management completely assign personnel to conduct DRRM activities to assure that risk of lives and property be reduced in times of calamity and that MDRRMO activities and necessary disaster preparedness could be undertaken and that MDRRMO be established in compliance with Sec. 2 and 1 of the IRR of RA 10121, respectively.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Construction of the building where the MDRRMO will be stationed was commenced during CY 2016.
observations.reason: Construction of the building where the MDRRMO will be stationed was commenced during CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management to track the implementation of these projects in accordance with DILG/DBM JMC No. 2011-1, and require the concerned LGU officials to submit explanation on the delayed project implementation that likewise cause delay in the delivery of benefits to the target beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Some of the projects were still not commenced by the LGU because of time-Bids constraints.
observations.reason: Time-constraint and lack of funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management formulate a Gender and Development Plan for the ensuing year and allocate funds equivalent to five percent (5%) of the total budget appropriation for the implementation thereof.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 GAD Plan was formulated by management.
observations.reason: PAPs were still incorporated in the various offices.

observations.recommendation: a) We recommend that Management require the production of P176,290.14. b) We also recommend that Management request refund of P327,332.75 arising from disallowances. c) We recommend further that Management locate all vouchers as these will become permanently disallowed otherwise. d) Lastly, we recommend that Management initiate the conduct of investigation whether or not the acts of Mr. Tabigue warrant the filing of administrative and/or criminal charges, otherwise, refer the investigation to the Office of the Ombudsman for the Visayas.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Demand letters were served. Demand letters and partial payments were made. The D.O. was relieved from his position and eventually forced to resign. Partial settlement was made.
observations.reason: Demand letters were served. Partial payments were made.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned municipal officials particularly the Municipal Accountant and the Disbursing Officer see to it that rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and replenishment of the Petty Cash Fund should be strictly adhered to; limit the disbursement of the fund to petty operating expenditures and it should not be used to pay regular and recurring expenses. Transactions which amount is more than P1,000.00 should not be paid out of the fund but by check as provided under existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 COA recommendation enforced; specific personnel were tasked to review on this matter in the Municipal Accountant’s Office.
observations.reason: COA recommendation enforced; specific personnel were tasked to review on this matter in the Municipal Accountant’s Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management adhere to existing guidelines in the payment of PEI and other benefits for LGU personnel to avoid disallowances in audit.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Management recognized the COA findings and made some counter actions/clarifications on the matter.
observations.reason: PEI still granted despite exceeding the PS limit.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: a) Require all officials and concerned employees authorized the passenger/s to ensure that expenses stated. b) Require the Municipal Accountant to verify and ascertain that payments for gasoline, oil and lubricants are duly supported with completely filled up driver’s trip tickets for proper evaluation on the propriety and reasonableness of fuel consumption. c) Prepare and submit to the Auditor on a monthly basis, the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for each vehicle for proper review and analysis.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Management already made a system to control the use and payments of these expenses. A single supplier was awarded through bidding for the purpose of purchasing the stated items.
observations.reason: Management made a system to control the use and payments and a single supplier was awarded through bidding for purchasing the stated items.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management stop the practice of charging prior year’s expenditures against current year’s appropriations and ensure that unpaid obligations/expenses as of year-end should be recorded as Accounts Payable in order to have a fair presentation of the financial statement at year end. The Municipal Budget Officer should not certify the Obligation Request unless there is an appropriation for the purpose. The Municipal Accountant should not certify as to availability of unobligated allotments if there is no appropriation for the purpose.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management stop the practice. Proper coordination was done to have expenditures obligated and recorded in the year they were incurred.
observations.reason: The management adhered to the recommendation; proper coordination was done so expenditures were recorded in the year incurred.
